facts.text: Pelican Bay was originally called Clam Bay before 1974, when the name changed to honor its abundant wildlife.
The community’s roots trace back to the early 1970s, when Coral Ridge Properties and the Barron Collier family developed 2,330 acres of land.
From the start, planners set aside mangroves, wetlands, and open space, and in 1982 the local mangrove forest became a protected natural resource area.
The Pelican Bay Foundation, the master homeowners’ association, maintains the common areas, keeping the community’s ecology and amenities in balance.
Pelican Bay is part of the Naples–Marco Island Metropolitan Statistical Area.
Geographically, Pelican Bay sits west of the Gulf of Mexico, north of Vanderbilt Beach Road, south of Naples, and east of U.S. 41 the Tamiami Trail.
